chunks.text: DEEPAK KUMAR Delhi,Indiadeepakdeshwal85@gmail.com+91-9868402577in/deepak-kumar-94285b159 SUMMARY Senior Java Backend Engineer with 18+ years of experience designing, developing, and scaling enterprise-grade REST APIs, cloud- native microservices, and distributed systems. Currently leading a team of 10 at Fidelity International, managing 150+ production APIs serving millions of financial transactio

national, managing 150+ production APIs serving millions of financial transactions on AWS and Kubernetes. Proven expertise in cloud migration (Cloud Foundry to AWS/Kubernetes), API performance optimization, CI/CD automation, and infrastructure cost reduction. AWS Certified Developer – Associate. ead backend or platform engineering roles in global product companies. s, serving 10+ downstream consumer applications handling millions of daily financial data requests. •Led end-to-end cloud migration of 150+ APIs from Cloud Foundry (PCF) to AWS + Kubernetes over 12 months, eliminating legacy PaaS dependency, reducing infrastructure hosting costs by ~30%, and improving deployment frequency from bi-weekly to on- demand daily releases. •Implemented Infrastructure as

ncy from bi-weekly to on- demand daily releases. •Implemented Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform and Kustomize across 3 environments (dev, staging, production), reducing manual deployment errors by ~80% and cutting new environment provisioning time from 3 days to under 2 hours. •Built and maintained CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and GitHub Actions for 150+ microservices, automating build

elines using Jenkins and GitHub Actions for 150+ microservices, automating build, test, containerization, and deployment to AWS EKS — achieving ~60% reduction in deployment cycle time and enabling zero-downtime production releases. •Drove API performance optimization across 150+ endpoints, achieving ~40% reduction in average response latency through query optimization, connection pooling, and cach

verage response latency through query optimization, connection pooling, and caching strategies, directly reducing monthly AWS compute costs by ~25%. •Built polyglot backend services using Java Spring Boot, Python, and Node.js to support 10+ downstream consumers with varying protocol and format requirements, reducing new consumer onboarding time by ~50% per integration. •Facilitated 10+ technical w

w consumer onboarding time by ~50% per integration. •Facilitated 10+ technical workshops and architecture review sessions with stakeholders across 3+ business units, onboarding 5+ new consumer applications and accelerating the cloud modernisation roadmap by 6+ months. •Advised cross-functional teams on cloud-native architecture, AWS security best practices (IAM, VPC, Secrets Manager), and cost opt

hitecture, AWS security best practices (IAM, VPC, Secrets Manager), and cost optimization strategies, maintaining 99.9% uptime SLAs across the global investment platform. ournal matching, exception grouping, and audit trail generation across 5+ derivative product types, reducing manual reconciliation effort by ~70%. •Implemented AutoSys batch job scheduling to orchestrate 10+ dependent jobs across data ingestion, reconciliation processing, and exception reporting — improving settlement processing speed by ~40% and eliminating manual scheduling errors. •Developed Pe

processing speed by ~40% and eliminating manual scheduling errors. •Developed Perl ETL scripts for file-based integration between the reconciliation system and 3+ upstream trading platforms, enabling automated daily data feeds without manual intervention. •Collaborated with operations, business analysts, and compliance teams across multiple geographies to validate reconciliation rules and ensure a

teams across multiple geographies to validate reconciliation rules and ensure adherence to OTC derivatives post-trade regulatory standards. •Led end-to-end UAT, performance testing, and production rollout of the mission-critical reconciliation system, ensuring zero data discrepancy in regulated settlement reporting. oftware Engineer NexGen Consultancy December 2009 – December 2011 •Designed and developed a full-cycle billing solution for 3+ state electricity boards, covering end-to-end workflows: meter reading collection, bill calculation, generation, deposit management, and automated reporting — processing billing data for thousands of consumers across multiple billing cycles. •Built JAX-WS (Metro) SOAP web

nds of consumers across multiple billing cycles. •Built JAX-WS (Metro) SOAP web services to integrate the billing module with 5+ external heterogeneous systems, enabling cross- platform data exchange and reducing manual reconciliation effort by ~60%. •Designed and optimised SQL Server stored procedures to handle high-volume billing transactions with accuracy during peak billing periods across mult
